$comment: Wi-Health RBAC contract — single source of truth for roles, permissions and token claims. Firmware, mobile (Flutter), admin (React), NestJS backend and Firebase rules must all agree with this file.

app_user.description: Patient or caregiver — identical capabilities. Access is scoped to the devices linked to the account.

admin.description: Fleet operator — uses the web admin panel only. Never logs into the mobile app.

device.description: Not a human role. ESP32-S3 sensors authenticate with a custom token minted by the backend (uid = deviceId, claim device=true) and may only write their own paths.

tokenClaims.$comment: Firebase custom claims set by the NestJS backend (Admin SDK) — never writable by clients. Rules check auth.token.role / auth.token.device.
tokenClaims.device: true (only on device custom tokens; human tokens omit it)

provisioning.signup: Client registers via Firebase Auth (email+password). Backend onUserCreate hook (or first-login endpoint) sets claim role=app_user and creates /users/$uid.
provisioning.promoteAdmin: Backend-only endpoint (existing admin required) sets claim role=admin. There is no client path to change roles.
provisioning.device: Admin registers a device in the panel; backend mints a long-lived custom token with uid=deviceId, device=true; token is flashed to the ESP32-S3 during provisioning.
provisioning.assignment: Admin links deviceId -> patient -> App User account by writing /users/$uid/devices/$deviceId=true and /devices/$deviceId/meta (see rtdb-structure.json). These links power the mobile patient switcher.

mobilePolicy: The mobile app requires permission viewOwnPatients (role app_user). Admin credentials are rejected client-side after authentication AND have no readable data paths on mobile flows server-side.
adminPanelPolicy: The React admin panel requires role=admin in token claims; route guard redirects non-admins to login with an error.
